response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine geospatial domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map, and has demonstrated ability to work with high-throughput distributed systems, but the role's core requirement — production-grade C++ or Rust systems programming — is not met. His C++ exposure is limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments writing IDE components in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis project; his primary commercial stack for the past decade is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no Rust experience and no routing tile pipeline or navigation guidance system background anywhere in the resume.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform) and Green Map, directly relevant to the geospatial data processing context of the role
Demonstrated distributed systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O, caching, and high-performance data stores
Long track record of working across multiple paradigms and languages (DLang, Node.js, C++, Swift, Java), suggesting adaptability and willingness to expand expertise
concerns: C++ experience is shallow and dated — a six-month stint in 2014 and a thesis project — far below the 'modern C++ in commercial environments' bar; no Rust experience at all
No routing tile processing, navigation pipeline, or navigation guidance system experience anywhere in the resume
Current and recent roles are firmly in TypeScript/Node.js web and AI tooling, indicating significant reskilling would be required to become productive in a C++ systems pipeline role
